ops.title: codex-wrapper agents may return \"Waiting for background task ... to complete\" as their final answer instead of the codex reply — instruct foreground exec and reply verification
ops.body:
In a workflow refuter stage, a `codex:codex-wrapper` agent returned the literal string "Waiting for background task `btc4xe3af` to complete before reading the codex reply." as its final result — the wrapper had launched `codex exec` in the background and ended its turn before the reply file existed (observed 2026-07-14, cc-notes Phase 1 storage-core workflow).

**Why:** the wrapper runs codex via Bash and can end its turn while the background task is pending; the workflow then treats the placeholder text as the stage result, silently dropping the entire review lane.

**How to apply:** every codex-wrapper / Skill(codex) prompt that must deliver a verdict says: run codex in the FOREGROUND, block until exec completes, never return while a background task is pending, and verify the reply text actually addresses the question (re-run once if empty/stale/off-topic). Related: [[codex-tmp-reply-files-can-collide]], [[codex-skill-stub-from-schema-wrappers]].
